content: The MediSync Model X1 is a volumetric infusion pump. Battery life is 8 hours on a full charge. The flow rate range is 0.1 to 999 mL/hr. Unit weight is 2.3 kg. It ships with firmware v4.2. Operating temperature range is 5 to 40 degrees Celsius.
The MediSync Model X2 is a portable patient monitor. Battery life is 12 hours. It measures ECG, SpO2, and NIBP. Unit weight is 1.8 kg. It runs firmware v2.7 and has a 10-inch touchscreen display.

The MediSync Model X3 is a portable ventilator. Battery life is 6 hours. Tidal volume range is 50 to 2000 mL. Unit weight is 8.5 kg. It ships with firmware v1.9. Respiratory rate range is 4 to 60 breaths per minute.

The MediSync Model S1 is a syringe pump for low-volume delivery. Battery life is 10 hours. The flow rate range is 0.01 to 200 mL/hr. Unit weight is 1.5 kg. It ships with firmware v3.1. It accepts 5, 10, 20, and 50 mL syringes.

The MediSync Model M1 is a multiparameter monitor. Battery life is 14 hours. It measures ECG, SpO2, NIBP, temperature, and CO2. Unit weight is 2.1 kg. It runs firmware v5.0 and has a 12-inch touchscreen display.

For the X1 and S1 pumps: Error E-101 indicates an occlusion was detected; clear the line and resume. Error E-205 indicates low battery; connect to AC power within 30 minutes. Error E-412 indicates air in the line; purge the tubing. Error E-530 indicates the pump door is open; close and latch it.

For the X2 and M1 monitors: Error E-101 indicates an ECG lead is off; reattach the lead. Error E-208 indicates an SpO2 sensor fault; reseat the SpO2 sensor. Error E-315 indicates an NIBP cuff error; check the cuff connection and size.

For the X3 ventilator: Error E-110 indicates high airway pressure; check for obstruction. Error E-220 indicates low battery; connect to AC power within 20 minutes. Error E-440 indicates a circuit disconnect; reconnect the breathing circuit.
The standard warranty is 24 months from the date of purchase. Returns are accepted within 30 days provided an RMA number is obtained. Defective units are replaced within 5 business days.

The extended warranty adds 12 additional months for $499. The premium service plan adds 24 additional months for $899 and includes on-site service with a 4-hour response time. Service plans must be purchased within 90 days of the device purchase.
Replacement tubing sets, SpO2 sensors, and NIBP cuffs are ordered through the customer portal. Standard reorder lead time is 7 business days. Bulk orders over 50 units receive a 10% discount.

The X1 and S1 infusion pumps must not be used with patients under 5 kg. Maximum continuous infusion time is 72 hours, after which the tubing must be replaced. In the ICU, alarm volume must be set above 60 dB. Adverse events must be reported within 24 hours.

The X3 ventilator must not be used with patients under 10 kg. Maximum continuous ventilation time before a circuit change is 168 hours. The device is MRI unsafe. Keep oxygen sources away from open flame and document FiO2 settings every hour.

The X2 and M1 monitors are MRI unsafe and must be kept outside the MRI suite. Leads are defibrillation-proof. Place ECG electrodes per the standard 5-lead configuration. Replace electrodes every 24 hours of continuous monitoring.
Clean pump exteriors using 70% isopropyl alcohol wipes. Do not autoclave any MediSync device. Replace the pump battery every 24 months. Annual preventive maintenance is required for all pumps.

Calibrate the NIBP cuff every 12 months. Perform an SpO2 sensor accuracy check every 6 months. Replace the monitor battery every 24 months. Calibration must be performed by a certified biomedical technician.
All MediSync devices are classified under ECCN EAR99. Export to embargoed countries is prohibited. Distributors must retain compliance records for 5 years. Re-export requires written authorization.

MediSync devices carry CE marking valid in the EU and are FDA 510(k) cleared in the US. The quality system is ISO 13485 certified. Devices comply with IEC 60601 electrical safety standards. Health Canada licenses are held for all models.

Devices export data via HL7 and FHIR interfaces. Validated integrations exist for Epic and Cerner. Wireless connectivity uses 802.11 a/b/g/n. Manual data export is available in CSV format. Integration requires a site connectivity assessment.

Operators must complete a 4-hour certification course before independent use. Recertification is required annually. Training covers device setup, alarm management, and cleaning. A train-the-trainer program is available for large sites.
